titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/287

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=738 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:24:16.076054+00 | translation moa=739 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:24:14.846613+00

p.cl:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis et fidelibus presentibus et futuris litteras istas uisuris uel audituris Walterus de
Ridal’ filius patricii de Ridal’ salutem in domino .

me dedisse concessisse et hac mea presenti carta
confirmasse deo et ecclesie beate Marie de melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus in liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosi
nam quandam partem terre mee in territorio de lilliscliue cum Toftis et Croftis que sunt inter terram Rogeri de Neu
botl’ et terram quam dedi Alexandro de Askirke scilicet totam terram quam matildis chorbeth tenuit in duarium in lintedikes
et benelandes et brunerig  in duobus locis . et in kaueres . et chengisflat . Et pratum quod dicitur stobimedue . et quinque acras
et unam percatam de dominico meo subtus harekamb ex australi parte . scilicet de terra quam alexander filius thoc de me tenuit
ad firmam . Terras istas et quicquid in eis continetur dedi predictis monachis . Et uolo ut eas cum predicto prato et cum omnibus commu
nibus aisiamentis eiusdem uille habeant teneant et possideant in perpetuum ita libere quiete plene et honorifice  sicut ali
quam elemosinam liberius quiecius plenius et honorificencius tenent uel possident . Et ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus predictas terras cum predicto prato et adquietabimus ab omni terreno seruicio et consuetudine et exaccione se
culari et demanda erga dominum Regem et omnes alios in perpetuum .

Hiis testibus . Domino meo Willelmo de Vesci . Ricardo ger
nun . Roberto de Euurth . Ricardo de Nichol’ . Hugone de reuedene . Gileberto de Wittune . Willelmo filio alexanderi de
lilliscliue et multis aliis .
To all sons and faithful men of Holy Mother Church present and future who shall view or hear these letters Walter of Ryedale son of Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting.

to have given, granted, and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, a portion of my land in the territory of Lilliesleaf with the tofts and crofts which are between Roger of Newbattle's land and the land that I gave to Alexander of Ashkirk, that is, the whole land that Matilda Corbett held in dower in 'Lintedikes' and 'Benelandes' and 'Brunerig', in two places, both in 'Kaueres' and 'Chengisflat', and the meadow which is called 'Stobimedue', and five acres and one perch from my demesne below 'Harekamb' on the southern side, that is, from the land which Alexander son of Thoc held from me at ferme. These lands and whatever is contained in them I have given to the aforesaid monks. And I will that they have, hold and possess them with the aforesaid meadow and with all common easements of the same toun for ever, as freely, quitly, fully, and honourably as they hold or possess any alms freely, quitly, fully, and honourably. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant the aforesaid lands with the aforesaid meadow, and we shall acquit from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction and demand towards the lord King and everyone else for ever.
With these witnesses, my lord William de Vescy, Richard Gernun, Robert of Airth, Richar de Nichol', Hugh of Redden, Gilbert of Whitton, William son of Alexander of Lilliesleaf, and many others.
